response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and exceeds the experience requirement with 19 years of professional work, they fall critically short on core technical requirements. C++ proficiency is essential to the role, but their last meaningful C++ work was a 6-month contract in 2014-2015; their career since has been dominated by TypeScript/JavaScript/DLang. Industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443-4-1) and MQTT protocol are key responsibilities with no demonstrated expertise. The candidate's education is in Computer Science rather than Engineering (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech as specified). Though they excel in distributed systems and event-driven architecture, the specific C++ and industrial-domain gaps would require substantial ramp-up time incompatible with the mid-senior level expectation.
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience (19 years) with proven track record building high-performance, distributed systems at scale
Strong foundation in event-driven architectures, asynchronous patterns, and concurrent programming—demonstrated at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing capability through workshops, Refactoring Dojos, and teaching roles—aligns with team leadership expectations
concerns: Critical C++ expertise gap: only 6 months of professional C++ work in 2014-2015; career has been dominated by TypeScript, JavaScript, and DLang since then
No demonstrated experience with industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443-4-1), secure development lifecycle practices, or MQTT protocol—all core to the role
Education background is Computer Science and Fine Arts, not Engineering (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech required); no evidence of IoT, embedded systems, or industrial automation domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise and recent hands-on experience
Distributed systems and event-driven architecture background
